key_insight: Optimization can be viewed as a coupled geometric system where learning dynamics, transported distributions, and a time-varying Riemannian metric evolve together, with some difficulties invariant but others reducible by changing geometry.
engineering_relevance: For optimizer design, the framework clarifies which bottlenecks come from geometry mismatch and conditioning versus fundamental topological or critical-point invariants, giving a benchmark for adaptive methods and preconditioners.
method: Defines optimization geometrodynamics as joint evolution of parameters, particles, and a controlled metric.
Introduces dynamic geometric complexity as the minimum geometric cost needed to reduce an optimization difficulty observable.
Derives exact benchmark results for strongly convex quadratics under full positive-definite metric control, including affine-invariant distance to a low-condition-number set.
evidence_signal: The paper is theory-only and evaluates its claims through formal theorem statements and proofs rather than experiments or datasets.
watch_item: Practical use depends on specifying admissible metric families, curvature estimates, and discretization error bounds, which may limit direct implementability.
paper_title: Optimization Geometrodynamics: A Framework for Dynamic Geometric Optimization
